title: Seeded Recursive Backtracking Generation · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent accessibility club receives a different grid, seed, algorithm, input profile, display scale, assistance need, platform, and failure condition and transfers seeded recursive backtracking generation into a changed seeded iterative and recursive backtracker. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of seeded iterative and recursive backtracker with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, c…
objectives: Maintain a current cell, visited set, frontier stack or call path, reciprocal passages, and a progress invariant.
Enumerate in-bounds unvisited neighbors before randomized selection and retain deterministic ordering before shuffling.
Inject and record a dedicated random generator and seed without contaminating global random state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Backtracker Invariant
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab puzzle team handles case 7984 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate seeded backtracking progress, completion, topology, depth, and replay; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates.

Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-generation-validation-yw1elw
# Competency: maze-generation-validation.
# Case 4650: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it.
def evidence_maze_generation_validation_yw1elw(cells: int = 48, visited: int = 48, carved_edges: int = 47, stack_peak: int = 21, replay_match: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_yw1elw = "yw1elw-wle1wy-4650"
    assert evidence_variant_yw1elw
    evidence_axes_yw1elw = (
        "gridyw1elw",
        "topology4650",
        "searchwle1wy",
        "eventyw1elw4650",
        "access4650yw1elw",
        "recoverywle1wy4650",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_yw1elw)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or visited < 0 or carved_edges < 0:
        return False, visited, "generation-count"
    if visited != cells:
        return False, visited, "unvisited-cell"
    if carved_edges != cells - 1:
        return False, carved_edges, "tree-shape"
    if stack_peak < 1 or not replay_match:
        return False, stack_peak, "replay-invariant"
    assert visited == cells and carved_edges == cells - 1
    return True, visited, "seeded-backtracker"
